missed_actual_themes: Status of construction resumption at Perfect Day Mexico after environmental pause
Whether European yield headwinds are expected to fully reverse in 2027 (magnitude and timing)
Itinerary changes or initiative deferrals specifically due to higher fuel; harvesting efficiencies into 2027
North American consumers’ behavior in response to higher airfares on North America itineraries
overpredicted_themes: Capital allocation, buybacks, leverage/rating targets, and financing mix (unsecured vs ECA)
Detailed JV/TUI reconciliation and contribution modeling
West Coast Mexico demand/yield disruption quantification
Percent booked by quarter and APD splits beyond what was asked
Guidance sensitivity tables (fuel/FX/APD) and mid-year guide update triggers
Fleet deployment strategy and capacity allocation across regions
Source-market mix shifts and Caribbean capacity downside scenarios
summary: Coverage was solid (8/12) with strong matches on Mediterranean booking impacts, yield decomposition (hardware vs like-for-like), Perfect Day Mexico ramp, hedging, and loyalty/card monetization. Precision was moderate (68/110 useful), with many overpredictions on capital allocation/financing, JV/TUI details, and granular cost cadence that did not appear in the actual Q&A. Misses centered on Mexico construction status, 2027 recovery of Europe headwinds, fuel-driven itinerary/initiative adjustments, and North American airfare impacts.
